10.reason: Loop condition should be `i<=strlen(s)-1` to include the last character.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Loop condition is `i<strlen(s)-1`, which misses the last character of the string. Should be `i<strlen(s)` or `i<=strlen(s)-1`.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Loop condition should be `i<=strlen(s)-1` to include the last character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition i<strlen(s)-1 causes the last character of the string to be skipped. It should be i<strlen(s) or i<=strlen(s)-1 to ensure the last character is processed and printed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition i<strlen(s)-1 causes the last character of the input string to be skipped and never printed. It should be i<strlen(s) to ensure all characters are processed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Loop condition excludes the last character, so if the string ends with a non-'-' character, it won't be printed. Also, when accessing s[i+1], if i == strlen(s)-2, then i+1 is the last valid index, but if the string has only one character after '-', this may still miss printing the final character after expansion.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Loop condition excludes the last character, causing it to be omitted from output when there's no trailing '-']
